title: How Much Does It Cost to Start a Lash & Brow Studio?
content:
Starting a Lash & Brow Studio typically costs between $15,000 and $60,000, depending on your location, scale, and approach. Small footprint, specialized service, growing demand. Lash extensions at $150-$300 per full set with 2-3 hour application time. Brow lamination at $50-$100 in 30 minutes. The fastest-growing segment in beauty services.

Quick Cost Summary

Costs are estimates based on national averages.

Detailed Cost Breakdown

Equipment & Core Assets - $5,250 to $21,000

Primary equipment for your lash & brow studio. This is typically your largest single expense. Buy used where possible - commercial equipment at 40-60% of retail from closing businesses.

Facility or Vehicle - $3,750 to $15,000

Whether you need commercial space, a work vehicle, or a mobile setup. Prioritize function over appearance in year one.

Licensing, Permits & Insurance - $500 to $5,000

Business license, industry-specific permits, and insurance. Research your state and local requirements before spending on anything else.

Marketing & Launch - $500 to $3,000

Google Business Profile, website, initial advertising. Focus on channels where lash & brow studio customers actually search.

Working Capital - $2,250 to $9,000

Cash reserves for 2-3 months of operating expenses while building revenue. Under-capitalization kills more businesses than bad ideas.

Monthly Operating Costs

ExpenseLow EstimateHigh Estimate
Licensing, Permits & Insurance (est.)$42/mo$417/mo
Marketing & Launch (est.)$42/mo$250/mo
Working Capital (est.)$188/mo$750/mo
Total Monthly$272/mo$1,417/mo

What Most People Forget

Hidden costs that catch first-time lash & brow studio owners off guard.

Self-Employment Taxes (15.3% of net earnings)

15.3% of net earnings for Social Security and Medicare on top of income tax. Set aside 25-30% of every dollar.

Seasonal Revenue Variation (20-50% seasonal decline)

Most lash & brow studio businesses see 20-50% revenue swings between peak and slow periods. Save during peaks to cover fixed costs during dips.

Equipment Maintenance & Replacement ($1,000-$5,000/year)

Commercial equipment needs regular maintenance and eventual replacement. Budget accordingly from month one.

Insurance Premium Increases (5-15% annual increase)

Premiums rise 5-15% annually. Any claim triggers higher renewals. Shop 3-5 insurers every year.

The Small Costs That Add Up ($3,000-$10,000/year)

Software subscriptions, fuel, supplies, repairs - individually trivial, collectively $3,000-$10,000/year.

How Long Does It Take?

Plan for 4 to 12 weeks.

Business Setup (1-2 weeks): Form LLC, get insurance, obtain required licenses.

Equipment & Preparation (1-4 weeks): Acquire equipment, set up workspace or vehicle.

Marketing & First Clients (1-4 weeks): Launch online presence and secure first paying clients.

Growth (Months 2-9): Build to consistent revenue through referrals and marketing.

How Long Until You're Profitable?

Most lash & brow studio owners reach profitability within 3 to 9 months.

A lash & brow studio with $15,000-$60,000 in startup costs typically reaches monthly breakeven within 3-9 months. Track your monthly breakeven number from day one.

Typical Breakeven Timeline

PeriodStageRevenue vs. Costs
Months 1–3Launch & ramp-upOperating at a loss
Months 3–6Building momentumStill in the red
Months 6–9Approaching breakevenNarrowing the gap
Months 9–12Reaching profitabilityAt or near breakeven
Months 12+Growth phaseGenerating profit

Most lash & brow studio owners break even within 3–9 months.

First-Year Cash Flow Summary

CategoryLowHigh
One-Time Startup Costs$12,250$53,000
12 Months Operating Costs$3,264$17,004
Total First Year$15,514$70,004

How to Start for Less

Start Small and Scale with Revenue (Save 20-40% of startup costs)

Launch the minimum viable version of your lash & brow studio and upgrade only as revenue justifies it.

Buy Used Equipment (Save $2,000-$20,000)

Commercial equipment from closing businesses sells at 30-60% of retail.

Leverage Free Marketing First (Save $500-$3,000)

Google Business Profile, Nextdoor, Facebook groups, and word-of-mouth before paid ads.

Negotiate Everything (Save 5-15% on major expenses)

Lease terms, vendor pricing, insurance rates - all negotiable.

Track Every Dollar from Day One (Save Prevents $2,000-$10,000 in penalties)

Use QuickBooks or Wave from your first transaction. Clean books prevent tax surprises.

Tools & Resources

Accounting: QuickBooks - Track income, expenses, and taxes for your lash & brow studio.

Business Insurance: Next Insurance - General liability and professional coverage for lash & brow studio businesses.

Business Formation: LegalZoom - Form your LLC. Liability protection is essential.

Payments: Square - Accept card payments, send invoices. Free reader, no monthly fees.

Website: Squarespace - Professional site with services, pricing, and contact info for your lash & brow studio.

Payroll: Gusto - Payroll, tax withholding, and benefits when you hire employees.

Some links are affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.

Comparing Startup Costs

  • Nail Salon - Similar beauty service model with higher per-client revenue. Some studios combine both.
  • Hair Salon - Much higher startup costs. Lash studios are the lean alternative to a full salon.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does it cost to start a lash & brow studio?

Startup costs range from $15,000 to $60,000 depending on scale, location, and equipment choices. The low end is a bootstrapped solo operation; the high end is a fully equipped professional launch.

How much do lash & brow studio owners make?

Income varies by pricing, volume, and model. Solo operators typically earn $40,000-$100,000/year. Owners who hire and scale can earn $80,000-$200,000+.

Is a lash & brow studio profitable?

Yes - well-run lash & brow studio businesses generate 10-25% net margins once established. Profitability depends on pricing, cost control, and consistent volume.

Do I need a license for a lash & brow studio?

At minimum, a general business license ($50-$200). Some lash & brow studio businesses require industry-specific licenses or certifications. Check your state and local requirements.

How do I get customers for a lash & brow studio?

Google Business Profile, Nextdoor, local Facebook groups, and asking every satisfied customer for referrals. Most lash & brow studio businesses build a sustainable client base within 2-4 months.

How long does it take to start a lash & brow studio?

Plan for 4-12 weeks from decision to first revenue. Timeline depends on licensing, equipment acquisition, and client building.
